content.full_text:
September 30, 1992

Mr. Scott Hallabrin,
Acting General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA. 95804-0807

Re: Request for Confirmation of Telephonic Advice

Dear Mr. Hallabrin:

Southern California Gas Company and several other California energy utilities ("Utilities") are concerned that, as a result of the effects of Proposition 140, there will be many new legislators in Sacramento who are not familiar with issues affecting public utilities. These legislators, and particularly those members of committees who are assigned energy utility bills, will be faced with legislation dealing with intricate utility issues such as ratemaking and authority of the Public Utilities Commission.

In order to make new legislators more effective, the Utilities wish to sponsor and conduct a seminar to instruct legislators regarding some of the complicated utility-related issues and concepts that they will need to know. The Utilities anticipate that the seminar will last two days and will probably be held in a location outside of Sacramento.

We recently asked Colleen McGee of your Technical Assistance staff if the costs of the seminar, including instructional material given to each legislator, would be considered a reportable gift or, conversely, if the costs of the seminar would be non-reportable as informational material pursuant to Government Code Section 82028 (b) (1). Ms. McGee responded that the seminar costs, excluding meals, refreshments, and lodging, if any, would be informational material and, thus, not a gift. Her response assumed, of course, that the seminar was for bona fide educational purposes.

Mr. Scott Hallabrin  
September 30, 1992  
Page 2

We would appreciate written confirmation of Ms. McGee's advice so that we can provide it to prospective seminar attendees who may erroneously believe the seminar is a reportable gift.

In your response, please let us know if the answer to the above question would change if the Utilities created and funded a Section 501 (c) (3) tax exempt, non-profit corporation to conduct the educational seminars.

Thank you for your assistance.

Sincerely,

Joyce A. Padleschat  
Joyce A. Padleschat, Attorney for Southern California Gas Company
